message:
fix(runtime): precedence-based eviction, queued task abort, and sequenced proposal revert

Anti-entropy trust state:
- Replace BTreeMap first-key eviction with precedence_cmp()-based
  eviction when the trust record store reaches MAX_TRUST_RECORDS. The
  previous approach always evicted the lexicographically first key,
  which could discard high-value trust records in favor of retaining
  lower-value ones that happened to sort later alphabetically.

Lane scheduler:
- Add abort_queued_task() method that decrements the queued_count for
  a task class's resolved lane. When the queue drops to zero, clears
  first_queued_at_ms to prevent stale starvation detection. Includes
  test verifying sequential abort fully drains the queue counter.

Optimization governor:
- Add seq field to AppliedProposal to track insertion order explicitly.
  Replace .reverse() (which relied on HashMap iteration order, which
  is non-deterministic) with .sort_by(|a, b| b.seq.cmp(&a.seq)) so
  proposals are always reverted in last-applied-first order regardless
  of HashMap iteration order changes across Rust versions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
